content: Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__) to version control — add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove these files from the repository to keep it clean
Always verify all imported modules and dependencies exist before referencing them — missing files like database.py, models.py, schemas.py cause critical import errors and application failures
Always complete test files without truncation — incomplete files with truncated functions cause syntax errors and break test runs
Never submit PRs with failing tests or truncated files — complete all test implementations and ensure they pass before requesting review
Always ensure database migration enum values match model definitions exactly — migration used 'PLANNING' but model used 'planning', causing runtime failures
Use proper foreign key relationships in tests instead of hardcoded IDs — hardcoded values like venue_id=1, concert_id=1 don't test actual relationships and will fail with real database constraints
Always implement CSRF protection and XSS escaping when using HTMX forms — use Flask-WTF CSRF tokens on data-modifying endpoints (hx-post, hx-delete) and escape user content with {{ content|e }}

Mock chained method calls require explicit return_value assignment at each level — use mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value instead of mock_db.query().filter().first.return_value
Never manually manage database sessions by calling next(get_db()) and db.close() — use FastAPI dependency injection or proper session context management instead of unsafe manual session handling
Never mix framework-specific syntax between different frameworks — using Flask url_for() in FastAPI templates causes incompatibility errors
Never use CORS allow_origins=['*'] in production code — make CORS configuration environment-dependent or use specific origins for security
Test public interface methods rather than private methods — avoid testing private methods like _validate_venue_data() directly and instead test the public methods that use them
Remove unused imports to maintain clean code — importing EmailStr and validator from pydantic when validation is implemented as standalone methods violates code conventions
Make hardcoded business values configurable through constants or environment variables — avoid magic numbers like ticket prices and rates embedded in code
Use request body with proper schema instead of query parameters for POST endpoints — POST endpoint should use proper schema in request body rather than query parameters
Validate data integrity constraints at the model level — enforce business rules like preventing negative durations and implementing proper field validation
Never remove essential dependencies that are still in use — pytest, pydantic, and alembic were removed from requirements but tests still import pytest and models need pydantic for schemas
Use modern SQLAlchemy 2.0 DeclarativeBase instead of deprecated declarative_base() — import from sqlalchemy.orm and create class Base(DeclarativeBase)
Use FastAPI lifespan context manager instead of deprecated @app.on_event('startup') for FastAPI 0.104.1+ — the on_event decorators are being phased out

Never change frameworks without implementing the complete backend infrastructure — switching from FastAPI to Flask requires implementing all routes, models, and app setup before templates can function
Always implement pagination on collection GET endpoints — missing limit/offset parameters on GET /concerts/ endpoint violates architecture decisions and causes performance issues with large datasets
Implement foundational layers before dependent components — create models, services, and backend endpoints before writing tests or frontend code that depends on them

Always include referenced template files in PRs when implementing routes — referencing add_concert.html, view_setlist.html, add_venue.html, and venues.html templates without including them breaks the application
API endpoints should return JSON data following REST conventions — returning raw HTML from API endpoints violates standards and makes testing/maintenance difficult
Define shared Base classes in a central module like models/base.py rather than locally with declarative_base() — local definitions cause import issues when other models need the same Base
Integrate validation methods into models or remove them — defining validate_email() and validate_capacity() methods without connecting them to SQLAlchemy using @validates decorator creates dead code
Quality gate 'coverage' failed — {"percent": 0.0, "threshold": 70, "status": "fail"}
